course_title: Topics: Latin Americn Issues: God, Sex, and Crime
subject: LACS
course_description: Topics: Latin Americn Issues. Topics vary.
course_notes: (Same as REL 270 1, ANT 285 3, HIST 296 1) This course satisfies the Religion major requirement:- Tradition-Christianity, Region. Throughout history, acts of crime have served as a revealing measure of the social and cultural values that bind communities together. In New Spain (colonial Mexico), a society fundamentally organized around the defense and propagation of the Catholic faith, religious offenses posed a profound challenge to the very ideological foundations that legitimized Spanish rule. Such transgressions not only threatened the moral order but also exposed the deep-seated social tensions that shaped daily life in colonial Mexico. This course examines colonial society through the lens of criminality by exploring major offenses prosecuted by the Mexican Inquisition between 1520 and 1820, along with the specific historical contexts in which these trials unfolded. Our objective is not only to understand the motivations behind inquisitorial repression, but also to unravel the complex social dynamics of the colony, particularly as they intersected with enduring divisions of race, class, and gender.
